metadata.title: Operation Spalax Water and Wastewater System Attack Scenario
metadata.author: Cybersecurity Analyst

Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex Operation Spalax targets water and wastewater systems, employing a multi-stage attack plan involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Process Malware Repository Scheduled Task User Account File DNS Record Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility Malware Metadata (Mechanism) Application Log Content (Mechanism) Scheduled Task/Job Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism File Metadata (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) System Information Discovery Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather information about water and wastewater systems, including infrastructure, personnel, and potential vulnerabilities, using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers identify open ports and services on water and wastewater system networks by monitoring network traffic using reg.exe utility.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers develop custom malware to exploit identified vulnerabilities in the water and wastewater system.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers target specific employees with tailored phishing emails to gain access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers use identified vulnerabilities to execute malware on compromised systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to maintain persistent access to the system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers elevate privileges to gain control over critical systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attackers hide malicious code from detection by obfuscating it.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to guess or crack passwords to gain access to sensitive areas.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers map out the water and wastewater system's network topology.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers move laterally within the network using valid accounts.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate collected data via alternative protocols to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt water and wastewater services to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: Operation Spalax is a complex attack scenario targeting water and wastewater systems, involving multiple stages of reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
